refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall minimize energy consumption per ton moved to meet or exceed the customer-defined energy efficiency target.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the high-level FR into a measurable and actionable sub-FR focused on minimizing energy per ton moved aligns with the acceptance criteria and enables targeted design and verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure total energy used and tons moved over the evaluation period and verify energy_per_ton ≤ E_target.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor and report energy consumption accurately.
FR2: Optimize vehicle operational parameters to reduce energy usage.
FR3: Provide feedback and control to operators to maintain energy efficiency.

candidate_dps: DP1: Integrate energy monitoring sensors and telemetry system.
DP2: Implement adaptive vehicle control algorithms for energy optimization.
DP3: Develop operator feedback interface and teleoperation tasking for energy efficiency.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Integrate energy monitoring sensors and telemetry system; DP2: Implement adaptive vehicle control algorithms for energy optimization; DP3: Develop operator feedback interface and teleoperation tasking for energy efficiency.

potential_sources: Telemetry data shared between energy monitoring and operator feedback modules.
Control algorithms interacting with vehicle hardware and teleoperation commands.
coupling_check.notes: DPs are logically coupled but designed to minimize cross-dependencies by clear interface definitions.

assumptions_risks: Assumes accurate and reliable energy sensors are available and integrable.
Risk that control algorithms may conflict with safety constraints requiring coordination with Safety Services.
Operator feedback effectiveness depends on teleoperation network reliability.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicit coordination mechanisms with Safety Services DP to mitigate safety-related risks.

im_boundary.name: Vehicle
im_boundary.scope: Physical vehicle systems including energy sensors and control actuators.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Energy consumption and operational data sent to NGHE Solution and Teleoperation modules.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receive operational commands from Teleoperation module to optimize energy usage.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Exchange safety status and alerts with Safety Services to ensure safe energy optimization.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment of energy sensors and control logic; low diffusion as interfaces are well-defined.
Contains operator feedback and tasking logic but relies on Vehicle for physical control.
Monitors safety but limited direct control over energy optimization.
System-level coordination and KPI monitoring; interfaces with all modules.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Energy efficiency control is best contained within the Vehicle module to minimize diffusion and ensure tight integration with physical sensors and actuators.
containment_risks: Potential conflicts with Safety Services requiring careful interface management.
Risk of overloading Vehicle module with complex control logic affecting maintainability.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Evaluate splitting control logic between Vehicle and Teleoperation modules if complexity grows.

fr_dp_gate.reason: FR refinement is clear and measurable; selected DPs logically satisfy sub-FRs with manageable coupling.

diffusion_risks: Telemetry interface diffusion between Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.
SafetyAlert interface requires robust coordination to avoid conflicts.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Vehicle module shows strong containment for physical realization; interfaces are well-defined to limit diffusion.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by measuring energy metrics and monitoring interface data flows.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of physical sensing and control to Vehicle; operator feedback and teleoperation separated.

recommended_revisions: Define explicit interface protocols for SafetyAlert to mitigate safety risks.
Monitor complexity in Vehicle module control logic for potential refactoring.

decision.justification: While the decomposition and assignments are sound, safety-related coordination and interface protocols require explicit definition to mitigate high safety criticality risks.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor and report energy consumption accurately.
FR2: Optimize vehicle operational parameters to reduce energy usage.
FR3: Provide feedback and control to operators to maintain energy efficiency.

revise.why: To explicitly incorporate safety coordination mechanisms and refine control algorithms to comply with safety constraints.

assumptions: Energy sensors are accurate and integrable.
Teleoperation network is reliable for operator feedback.
Safety Services module can provide timely alerts and coordination.
open_risks: Potential conflicts between energy optimization and safety constraints.
Complexity growth in Vehicle control logic affecting maintainability.
evidence_needed: Validation of sensor accuracy and integration.
Simulation or testing of control algorithms under safety constraints.
Interface protocol definitions and safety alert response times.
